content: {"perspective": "Regulatory", "question": "Round 1 established that no authority has issued a formal therapeutic opinion on intermittent fasting, yet commercial IF apps (Zero, Fastic, Simple, LIFE Fasting Tracker) market themselves to users with metabolic syndrome and type 2 diabetes. What specific marketing claims have these commercial apps made about weight loss, blood-sugar control, insulin sensitivity, or cardiometabolic health, and have any been subject to FTC advertising-substantiation enforcement, FDA warning letters for unapproved disease claims, or ASA (UK Advertising Standards Authority) / MHRA adjudications for borderline medical device or health claim issues?", "search_queries": ["Zero intermittent fasting app health claim weight loss blood sugar marketing advertising", "FTC warning letter dietary supplement app health claim substantiation intermittent fasting", "FDA warning letter unapproved disease claim intermittent fasting time-restricted eating app", "UK ASA adjudication intermittent fasting weight loss diet app advertising claim", "Simple Fastic LIFE fasting app metabolic syndrome diabetes health claim regulatory"]}
